competencies.name: Statistical Reasoning
competencies.description: Applies statistical methods correctly; understands uncertainty quantification; draws sound inferences from data; identifies flaws in study design.
positive_indicators: correctly identifies sources of bias without prompting
quantifies uncertainty using confidence intervals or credible intervals
distinguishes between statistical and practical significance
identifies assumptions of a test and checks whether they hold
proactively mentions confounders or selection effects
negative_indicators: conflates p-value with probability that the null is true
cannot explain what a confidence interval means
over-claims certainty from observational data
ignores multiple comparisons when relevant

competencies.name: Research Methodology & Design
competencies.description: Designs rigorous studies; selects appropriate methods for the research question; anticipates threats to validity; pre-registers or documents hypotheses before data collection.
positive_indicators: defines the estimand precisely before choosing a method
discusses internal and external validity
performs power calculation before collecting data
identifies potential confounders in study design
discusses pre-registration or hypothesis documentation
negative_indicators: selects a method before defining the research question clearly
ignores external validity of findings
does not discuss sample size justification
cannot explain what p-hacking or HARKing is

competencies.name: Model Development & Validation
competencies.description: Builds mathematical or statistical models for real-world phenomena; validates models against empirical data; understands when models break down.
positive_indicators: starts with a simple baseline before adding complexity
validates model assumptions with diagnostic checks
discusses where the model is likely to fail
compares model predictions to out-of-sample data
interprets model parameters with domain context
negative_indicators: skips baseline models and jumps to complex approaches
cannot describe how to validate model assumptions
presents model output without discussing limitations
conflates model fit on training data with predictive performance

competencies.name: Critical Thinking & Assumptions
competencies.description: Challenges assumptions explicitly; evaluates evidence critically; identifies logical gaps; updates beliefs when presented with new data.
positive_indicators: states assumptions explicitly before building on them
challenges their own conclusions when probed
identifies alternative explanations for observed effects
updates position clearly when given contrary evidence
negative_indicators: makes hidden assumptions without stating them
defends a position without engaging with counter-evidence
cannot identify what would falsify their hypothesis
treats correlation as confirmation of a causal hypothesis

competencies.name: Communication of Quantitative Work
competencies.description: Explains complex statistical results to mixed audiences; uses visualizations effectively; communicates uncertainty honestly; writes clear technical reports.
positive_indicators: explains statistical results in plain language without losing precision
uses concrete examples and analogies for abstract concepts
represents uncertainty visually, not just as numbers
leads with the conclusion and business implication
negative_indicators: buries the main finding in technical details
reports point estimates without uncertainty ranges
cannot simplify for a non-technical executive audience
uses jargon without checking whether the audience follows

competencies.name: Domain Knowledge & Literature
competencies.description: Aware of relevant prior work; understands domain-specific data generation processes; can contextualize findings within the broader research landscape.
positive_indicators: references relevant prior work and situates their approach
understands how the data was generated and what biases that introduces
identifies domain-specific pitfalls in applying standard methods
negative_indicators: applies methods without awareness of domain-specific constraints
unaware of relevant literature when asked
ignores how data collection process affects analysis
